zh.Standish, Lancashire, England: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾斯坦迪什
zh.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mules於1243年出生在英國藍卡爾斯坦迪什。她於1264年與John d'Euxton在英國英格蘭藍卡爾斯坦迪什結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國藍卡爾艾斯頓。

zh-hans.Standish, Lancashire, England: 英国英格兰藍卡爾斯坦迪什
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mules于1243年出生在英国藍卡爾斯坦迪什。她于1264年与John d'Euxton在英国英格兰藍卡爾斯坦迪什结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国藍卡爾艾斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mules
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: (i) variant of Mullis . (ii) variant of Moles (2). (iii) variant of Mule with post-medieval excrescent -s. Compare John Meul, 1327 in Subsidy Rolls (Newlyn East, Cornwall).

variants.name: Subsidy Rolls
meanings: English: (i) occupational name from northern Middle English * gater ‘goatherd’, a derivative of Middle English gayte ‘goat’ (from Old English gāt, with preservation of the unrounded northern ā, or from Old Scandinavian geit). See also Goater . The name is found in N England and the Midlands, and has probably also absorbed Middle English gaythird ‘goatherd’, as in John le Gaythirde, 1301 in Subsidy Rolls (Yorks), Robert Gayterd, 1466 in York Freemen’s Register . (ii) locative name from Middle English * gater ‘dweller by the town gate or gateway’, see Gate . This type of name, derived from a topographical term + -er, is mainly found in S England, especially Kent, Sussex, and Hants.

variants.name: Mule
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Angelo, Antonio, Matteo, Sal, Carmelo, Concetta, Francesco, Gaetano, Gaspare, Gino, Rosario.
English: nickname, perhaps for a stubborn person, from Middle English mule, muel, meul, moile ‘mule’ (Old French mule). See also Moyle and Moule , with which this name may have been interchangeable.

variants.name: Moles
meanings: English: variant of Moll , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Catalan: habitational name from any of various minor places called Moles, from the plural of mola (see Mola ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Meulles (Calvados). See also   Moyles  .

variants.name: Mullis
meanings: variant of Moule , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

habitational name from Mowlish Farm in Kenton (Devon). The placename appears to mean ‘farm of the mules’, from the genitive plural form of Old English mūl ‘mule’ + hīwisc ‘family, measure of land that would support a family’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mules was born in 1243, in Standish, Lancashire, England. She married John d'Euxton in 1264, in Standish, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in Euxton, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mules est née en 1243, à Standish, Lancashire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé John d'Euxton en 1264, à Standish, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Euxton, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mules wurde 1243 in Standish, Lancashire, England geboren. Sie hat 1264 in Standish, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom John d'Euxton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Euxton, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mules è nata il nel 1243 nel Standish, Lancashire, England. Lei sposato John d'Euxton nel 1264, nel Standish, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel Euxton, Lancashire, England.

ja.Euxton, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアエウクストン
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mulesは1243年にイギリスランカシュアイアスタンディッシュで生まれた。1264年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアスタンディッシュでJohn d'Euxtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスランカシュアイアエウクストンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mules(은)는 1243년 영국 랭카실르 스탠디시에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1264년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 스탠디시에서 John d'Euxton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 랭카실르 우스턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mules nasceu em 1243, em Standish, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com John d'Euxton em 1264, em Standish, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Euxton, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mules родилась в 1243 г. в местности стандиш, ланкашир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John d'Euxton в 1264 г. в местности стандиш, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности эукстон, ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret de Mules nació en 1243, en Standish, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Se casó con John d'Euxton en 1264, en Standish, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en Euxton, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
